title: Testing Your Code
knowledge_note: Muslim engineers tested their machines before use. A program without tests is a bridge without inspection — you never know when it will fail.
explanation: A 'test' block checks that your code does exactly what you expect. Run tests to catch mistakes before your users do.
starter_code:
test "doubling works":
    remember result = 2 * 21
    check result is 42

test "strings work":
    remember s = "hello"
    check s.upper() is "HELLO"
worked_example:
test "doubling works":
    remember result = 2 * 21
    check result is 42

test "strings work":
    remember s = "hello"
    check s.upper() is "HELLO"
challenge: Write a test for a recipe you created.
hint: Write a recipe, then write test "my recipe works": and check its output.
